title: Man allegedly kills man in R.I. before causing car crash that killed 2 in Mass.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Demitri Sousa, 28, is charged with murder after allegedly shooting Javon Lawson, 35, in Cranston, Rhode Island.
Sousa allegedly caused a fatal car crash in Swansea, Massachusetts, killing two people after the Rhode Island shooting.
Police found a pistol and ammunition in Sousa's vehicle; he was hospitalized and remains in police custody.

title: Weighing the pros and cons of a Robert Thomas trade for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins are considering trading for St. Louis Blues center Robert Thomas, a top-six star with over 80 points recently.
General Manager Don Sweeney emphasized a cautious approach to trades, given the Bruins' need to manage future assets wisely.
Acquiring Thomas would likely require Boston to give up key draft picks and prospects, posing a significant cost to the team's future.

title: An update: Did a Brooklyn couple kill a neighbor’s trees for a better view in Maine?
author: New York Times Service
bullets: The Maine Board of Pesticides Control approved a $3,000 settlement for Stephen Antonson and Kathleen Hackett over tree poisoning.
The settlement did not require the Brooklyn couple to admit guilt for the trees' death in Rockport, Maine.
Neighbor Eric Grubman suggested a donation in honor of his late mother to Maine coastal preservation as compensation.

title: 10 combine standouts that could make sense for Patriots to select in April’s NFL Draft
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots eye prospects after strong performances at NFL Scouting Combine, needing depth at wide receiver and edge rusher.
Edge rushers Romello Height, Dani Dennis-Sutton, and Malachi Lawrence impress, potentially fitting Patriots' Day 2 draft plans.
Wide receivers KC Concepcion and Jeff Caldwell show promise, with Caldwell's speed and size boosting his draft stock.

title: Insider: Patriots probably won’t make a ‘Micah Parsons-type’ trade this offseason
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are unlikely to trade significant draft assets for star players like A.J. Brown or Maxx Crosby this offseason.
Coach Mike Vrabel emphasizes building through the draft with 11 picks, mostly in later rounds, for sustainable success.
Despite having $40 million in cap space, a major move in free agency is also unlikely for the Patriots this offseason.

title: Largest energy storage facility statewide gets approved for construction in Everett
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jupiter Power secures approval to build Massachusetts' largest battery energy storage system at former ExxonMobil site in Everett.
The facility will feature 816 lithium-ion battery enclosures and connect to the existing Eversource Mystic Substation.
Project is part of a larger "Docklands" development, including manufacturing, lab, residential, and retail spaces on the site.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Connor Storrie hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Connor Storrie hosted 'SNL' on February 28 with Mumford & Sons as the musical guest.
Storrie's 'Heated Rivalry' co-star Hudson Williams appeared in a skating-themed sketch.
US Olympic hockey team members, including Megan Keller and Hilary Knight, appeared during the monologue.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s loss against the Red Bulls
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 1-0 to New York Red Bulls, marking their second consecutive defeat to start the 2026 season.
Revolution's defense, with returning players Brayan Ceballos and Mamadou Fofana, handled intense pressure but struggled with possession.
Missed second-half opportunity saw Jackson Yueill and Griffin Yow's failed coordination, thwarting their best chance to score.

title: Triston Casas reportedly unlikely to be ready by Opening Day, while Kutter Crawford, Patrick Sandoval take step forward in rehab
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Triston Casas likely won't be ready for Opening Day due to a patellar tendon injury but could play in spring training.
Kutter Crawford threw live batting practice for the first time since a wrist injury, aiming for a return by Opening Day.
Patrick Sandoval, post-Tommy John surgery, pitched multiple innings in simulated practice, hopeful to be ready for Opening Day.

title: Chicopee installs security cameras on school bus stop arms to enforce illegal passing laws
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chicopee launches a program installing cameras on school bus stop arms to deter illegal passing of stopped buses.
The system captures license plate numbers when drivers fail to stop, issuing citations without police presence.
Chicopee's initiative follows a pilot with high violation rates; over 3,400 illegal passes recorded in one district.
